key_hedging_phrases: actual results may differ materially from any future results expressed or implied by the forward-looking statements
Risks and uncertainties include without limitation
effects of global and regional economic conditions, including as a result of government policies, trade and other international disputes
key_bullish_phrases: September quarter revenue record of $102.5 billion, including a September quarter revenue record for iPhone and an all-time revenue record for Services
our most extraordinary lineup of products as we head into the holiday season
our installed base of active devices also reached a new all-time high across all product categories and geographic segments
record fiscal year, with revenue reaching $416 billion, as well as double-digit EPS growth
we are excited to be sharing our most extraordinary lineup of products
key_risk_phrases: effects of global and regional economic conditions, including as a result of government policies, trade and other international disputes, geopolitical tensions, conflict, terrorism, natural disasters, and public health issues

risks relating to the design, manufacture, introduction, and transition of products and services in highly competitive and rapidly changing markets, including from reliance on third parties for components, technology, manufacturing, applications, services, support, and content
effects of unfavorable legal proceedings, government investigations, and complex and changing laws and regulations
risks relating to information technology system failures, network disruptions, and failure to protect, loss of, or unauthorized access to, or release of, data
payload.management_tone_summary: Management's tone is notably more emphatic and superlative than the prior quarter, escalating from 'proud' and 'very pleased' to 'very proud' and 'most extraordinary lineup ever,' with particular emphasis on the all-time Services record and positioning for the holiday quarter. The language around the iPhone launch cycle is more effusive than Q3, explicitly framing the upcoming product portfolio as a catalyst heading into the seasonally strongest quarter. The forward-looking framing around the holiday lineup represents a mild but meaningful incremental bullishness relative to Q3.
payload.analyst_revision_rationale: The press release highlights multiple record-setting achievements — all-time Services high, September quarter iPhone record, all-time installed base high — entering the holiday quarter with what management describes as the company's 'most extraordinary lineup' ever, including iPhone 17, iPhone Air, AirPods Pro 3, M5 MacBook Pro, and iPad Pro. Analysts may be underestimating the cumulative upsell opportunity from a broad simultaneous product refresh cycle across multiple categories coinciding with the seasonally strongest quarter. The all-time installed base record is a structural Services revenue driver that many sell-side models may not fully capture in their FY2026 Services run-rate assumptions. Additionally, the incremental superlative language vs. Q3 ('most extraordinary' vs. 'double-digit growth' framing) suggests management has higher-than-usual internal confidence in holiday demand trends. The absence of any softening language around geographic headwinds or demand caution — which had been present in earlier quarters — further supports a modest upward revision bias to both Q1 FY2026 revenue and Services estimates.
